steps.title: Identify the pricing structure for each class
steps.content:
The promotion sets up three tiers:
  • Class 1: $0 (free)
  • Class 2: $26.40 ÷ 2 = $13.20 (half off)
  • Classes 3 onward: $26.40 each (regular price)

steps.title: Count regular-priced classes and build the formula
steps.content:
For $x$ classes where $x > 2$:
  • Classes 1 and 2 have special pricing
  • Classes 3 through $x$ cost $26.40 each
  • Count of regular-priced classes: $x - 2$

Total cost is the sum of class 2 and all regular-priced classes:
$$f(x) = 13.20 + 26.40(x - 2)$$
Rearranging:
$$f(x) = 26.40(x - 2) + 13.20$$

This matches choice B — B is correct.
